extraction.one_line_summary: JAKOBSEN Fabio won his second stage of the week from ACKERMANN Pascal's wheel after GILBERT Philippe delivered a perfect 1.5km leadout through a technical corner.
tactical_patterns: GILBERT Philippe's 1.5km leadout through the final corner positioned JAKOBSEN perfectly to jump ACKERMANN's wheel, revealing Quick-Step's corner timing advantage.
Team Sky allowed multiple early breaks as long as CAVAGNA Rémi stayed behind, showing 17-second overall gaps trigger defensive responses.
Quick-Step controlled closing stages despite ACKERMANN holding points jersey, indicating sprint confidence overrides jersey mathematics when form is strong.

rider_signals.JAKOBSEN Fabio: positive: Second win this week shows rookie sprinter peaks better in multi-stage blocks than one-off races
rider_signals.GILBERT Philippe: positive: 1.5km leadout length suggests veteran still has diesel power for extended pulls in hilly finishes
rider_signals.ACKERMANN Pascal: neutral: Led sprint first but got jumped from own wheel, showing leadout timing matters more than pure speed
rider_signals.CAVAGNA Rémi: neutral: Early break attempts shut down due to 17-second GC gap, marking him as threshold for Sky intervention
rider_signals.MOSCON Gianni: positive: Secured first World Tour stage race at 24 despite minimal commentary focus, suggesting controlled leadership
